title: Sorbus buekkensis
intro: Sorbus buekkensis, also known as the Bükkszéplaki or Hungarian Rowan, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the Rosaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers. Bioactive constituents found in Sorbus buekkensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Sorbus buekkensis include infusions, tinctures, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Sorbus buekkensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It belongs to the genus Sorbus, which includes other species of rowan and mountain ash trees. The species is native to Europe and is considered to be an endangered species. It is a deciduous tree that produces white flowers and red berries. Sorbus buekkensis is also known as Bükki rowan or Bükki mountain ash.
uses: Sorbus buekkensis has been traditionally used in folk medicine. The plant's leaves and bark are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It is often used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption. The potential medicinal uses of Sorbus buekkensis are still being studied and explored by scientists.
